title: Surgical instrument with rotary drive selectively actuating multiple end effector functions
abstract: Surgical instruments having a rotary drive (2730) hat can selectively actuate multiple end effector functions are described herein. In at least one embodiment, a surgical instrument includes a shaft (2200), an end effector (7000) coupled to the shaft, a rotatable drive shaft (2730) coaxially disposed within an inner lumen of the shaft, and a plurality of selectively engageable clutches (6110, 6210, 6310) configured to transfer torque from the rotatable drive (2730) shaft to the end effector (7000). Each of the clutches (6110, 6210, 6310) is configured to convert the rotation of the drive shaft (2730) into one of a plurality of movements of the end effector (7000) relative to the outer shaft.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A surgical instrument, comprising: an outer shaft; an end effector coupled to the outer shaft; a rotatable drive shaft coaxially disposed within an inner lumen of the outer shaft; and a plurality of selectively engageable clutches configured to transfer torque from the rotatable drive shaft to the end effector, wherein each of the plurality of clutches is configured to convert rotation of the drive shaft into one of a plurality of movements of the end effector relative to the outer shaft. 2. The surgical instrument of Claim 1 , wherein the plurality of movements of the end effector includes rotation of the end effector relative to the outer shaft. 3. The surgical instrument of Claim 2, wherein the plurality of movements of the end effector includes actuation of an end effector function. 4. The surgical instrument of Claim 3, wherein the end effector comprises first and second jaws configured to grasp tissue therebetween, and wherein actuation of the end effector function comprises moving the first and second jaws between an open configuration and a closed configuration. 5. The surgical instrument of Claim 1 , wherein the plurality of selectively engageable clutches comprises a first clutch and a second clutch, wherein the plurality of end effector movements comprises a first end effector movement and a second end effector movement, wherein the drive shaft drives the first end effector movement when the first clutch is drivingly engaged with the drive shaft, wherein the drive shaft drives the second effector movement when the second clutch is drivingly engaged with the drive shaft, wherein the surgical instrument further comprises a first lockout and a second lockout, wherein the first lockout is configured to lockout the second end effector movement when the first clutch is drivingly engaged with the drive shaft, and wherein 

 the second lockout is configured to lockout the first end effector function when the second clutch is drivingly engaged with the drive shaft. 6. The surgical instrument of Claim 1 , wherein each clutch includes an electromagnet. 7. The surgical instrument of Claim 6, wherein each clutch is in a disengaged state in the absence of current flow to the electromagnet. 8. The surgical instrument of Claim 7, further comprising a plurality of lockouts, wherein each lockout is configured prevent one of the end effector movements when the corresponding clutch is in its disengaged state. 9. The surgical instrument of Claim 1 , further comprising a non-rotating elongate member coaxially disposed within an inner lumen of the rotating drive shaft. 10. The surgical instrument of Claim 9, wherein each clutch includes an electromagnet disposed along the non-rotating elongate member. 1 1. The surgical instrument of Claim 10, wherein each clutch further comprises a permanent magnet disposed around the rotating drive shaft configured to translate proximally and distally relative to the rotating drive shaft. 12. A surgical instrument, comprising: an outer shaft; an end effector coupled to the outer shaft; a rotatable drive shaft coaxially disposed within an inner lumen of the outer shaft; a first selectively engageable clutch disposed along a distal portion of the rotatable drive shaft that is configured to transfer torque from the drive shaft to cause a first movement of the end effector; and 

 a second selectively engageable clutch disposed along a portion of the rotatable drive shaft that is proximal to the first clutch, the second clutch being configured to transfer torque from the drive shaft to cause a second movement of the end effector. 13. The surgical instrument of Claim 12, further comprising a third selectively engageable clutch disposed along a portion of the rotatable drive shaft that is proximal to the second clutch, the third clutch being configured to transfer torque from the drive shaft to cause a third movement of the end effector. 14. The surgical instrument of Claim 12, wherein the first movement is actuation of an end effector function. 15. The surgical instrument of Claim 14, wherein the end effector comprises first and second jaws configured to grasp tissue therebetween; and wherein actuation of the end effector function comprises moving the first and second jaws between an open configuration and a closed configuration. 16. The surgical instrument of Claim 14, wherein the second movement is rotation of the end effector. 17. The surgical instrument of Claim 16, further comprising a third selectively engageable clutch disposed along a portion of the rotatable drive shaft that is proximal to the second clutch, the third clutch being configured to transfer torque from the drive shaft to cause articulation of the end effector. 18. The surgical instrument of Claim 12, wherein each clutch includes an electromagnet. 19. The surgical instrument of Claim 18, wherein each electromagnet is disposed along a non-rotating elongate member coaxially disposed within an inner lumen of the drive shaft. 20. The surgical instrument of Claim 18, wherein each clutch further comprises a permanent magnet disposed around the rotating drive shaft and configured to translate proximally and distally relative thereto. 21. A surgical method, comprising: rotating a drive shaft within an inner lumen of an outer shaft that is coupled to an end effector; engaging a first clutch to transfer torque from the drive shaft and cause a first movement of the end effector; and engaging a second clutch to transfer torque from the drive shaft and cause a second movement of the end effector which is different than the first movement. 22. The surgical method of Claim 21 , further comprising engaging a third clutch to transfer torque from the drive shaft and cause a third movement of the end effector which is different than the first movement and the second movement. 23. The surgical method of Claim 21 , wherein the first movement is actuation of an end effector function. 24. The surgical method of Claim 23, wherein the end effector comprises first and second jaws configured to grasp tissue therebetween and actuation of the end effector function comprises moving the first and second jaws between an open configuration and a closed configuration. 25. The surgical method of Claim 23, wherein the second movement is rotation of the end effector. 26. The surgical method of Claim 25, further comprising engaging a third clutch to transfer torque from the drive shaft and cause articulation of the end effector. 27. The surgical method of Claim 21 , further comprising locking the end effector against a movement when the clutch that causes the movement is disengaged. 28. The surgical method of Claim 21 , wherein engaging any of the first clutch and the second clutch comprises actuating an electromagnet.
